entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Extensibility – Guide to extend API_BUSINESS_PARTNER API for Standard Fields
Re: How to scrape images from Google
entry.content:
I had to play around a little bit with the recording, not complicated but a few tweaks.

  • I did Recorded but manually captured any new screen that had some element I needed to manipulate. I ended up with 2 screens, the Google home page and the results page, and I had 3 captures on the results page: results, results for images, results for images with an image selected.
  • As far as Declared Elements, I had the following:
    • Google Home - Search box
    • Google Home: Google logo
    • Google Home: Google Search button
    • Results Page: All of the returned images, as collection (using class YQ4gaf)
    • Results Page: "Images" filter
    • Results Page: Enlarged image that appears when you click one of the images (using class sFlh5c FyHeAf iPVvYb)

Can't guarantee these classes are stable and will work tomorrow, but they worked now with multiple searches.

As for the Get Property, for your element you should be able to see the attributes the automation finds (in criteria screen) and these should be available.

entry.title: Re: Connection between BTP and SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Harry, could you be more specific on what has been done already on your side (what configuration) and did you have a success? I expect you deploy your solution (static approuter with SAPUI5 application) into BTP Cloud Foundry? What needs to be done is, that you deploy solution first to get approuter URL. This URL then needs to be maintaned in Provisioning of SuccessFactors. Once you reach your approuter URL, you'll get multiple options for authentication (based on xsuaa service configuration taken from xs-security.json) and you should be able to see SuccessFactors IdP in there. After that, redirect user to a route where SAPUI5 application is sitting (based on xs-app.json configuration) and try to call a route that points to a destination, where SuccessFactors API is configured.

Do not forget to create 'client oauth2 application' in SuccessFactors (application 'Manage oauth2 client application').

Useful link:
https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/user-propagation-from-cloud-foundry-environment-to-sap-successfactors

entry.title: Re: Equivalent ABAP 7.4 code for the below code (loop and...
entry.content:
I know this is probably not correct but this is the approach we went with:

 TYPES: ty_old_doc TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF vbeln WITH DEFAULT KEY.
      DATA(doc_num) = VALUE ty_old_doc( FOR wa_accit IN t_accit WHERE ( old_doc_number IS NOT INITIAL ) ( wa_accit-old_doc_number ) ).
      IF LINE_EXISTS( t_vbrp[ vbeln = doc_num[ 1 ] ] ).
        lv_tdname = t_vbrp[ vbeln = doc_num[ 1 ] ]-aubel.
      ENDIF.

Not saying it is the only option or it is the correct equivalent but simply saying that it works the expected way. The salient difference being in the original code, doc_num was just a variable of char type. In the code we went with, doc_num is a table.

entry.title: Re: SAP Personas , script OnEnter with unwanted "enter" f...
entry.content:
Hello Christin,

The "return true" works on the simple flavor but not on the complex one.

I will try to figure out.

I need to use the Enter event as my users scans the EAN and all barcode are  setup with Enter function.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: 999 – The Number of the BSEG
entry.content: Just for those interested: With the note 3527363, an intercompany invoice posted via IDOC can also be summarized BSEG-wise by implementing the BAdi BADI_FINS_SUMMARIZE_BSEG. See Juan Pedro's request above.

entry.title: Re: How to change the default values for the crtl+f "Find...
entry.content:
Hello Birgir,

the settings from the find dialog are persisted on your local client.
There is a configuration file called settings.xml in your user profile under %APPDATA%\SAP\SAP GUI\ABAP Editor

This file contains this section:

<FindReplace>
<SearchFlags>0</SearchFlags>
<MatchCase>0</MatchCase><MatchWholeWord>0</MatchWholeWord><RegExp>0</RegExp><DirectionUp>0</DirectionUp><SearchHiddenText>0</SearchHiddenText><ScopeSelection>0</ScopeSelection><IncrementalSearch>0</IncrementalSearch></FindReplace>

And here the settings are persisted.
I could imagine that the editor cannot write to the settings.xml for some reason and that therefore the settings are getting lost.

Best regards,
Frank

entry.title: Re: Abap how to display internal table content in the right container as a HTML page.
entry.content: To add code: click on ... then on </> , paste the code without formatting

entry.title: Re: Impact on ESS/MSS apps ECC to S/4 Hana
entry.content: Please reply ...

entry.title: Re: Can I create Adaptation Project from my custom Fiori...
entry.content: why bother? its your own probject, cann't you just change it directly?

entry.title: Site Menu in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
entry.content:
The Site Menu in SAP Build Work Zone is more than a navigation tool; it serves as the gateway for users to access a wide range of features, applications, and resources on the platform. Understanding how to configure, customize, and manage the Site Menu is essential for administrators aiming to create a seamless, role-specific, and intuitive user experience while enabling an efficient digital workspace.

In SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, administrators have various options to add, organize, and manage menu items, tailoring navigation to meet the unique needs of different user groups. This blog post provides an overview of these capabilities.

 

Overview

The Site Menu in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, serves as the primary navigation aid for users, providing them with quick access to workspaces, applications, and key resources. A well-organized Site Menu enhances user experience by making navigation intuitive and reducing the time it takes for users to find what they need. Users can access the menu, by the small edit icon on the right side of the top navigation bar.

 

How to configure site menu capabilities

Configuring the Site Menu involves a few essential steps:

Accessing the Site Menu Configuration: Company administrators can access the Site Menu configuration panel directly within the SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition interface. From here, they can review the current menu structure and make the necessary adjustments.

Editing Menu Items: Administrators have full control over the menu, allowing them to add, remove, disable, duplicate or reorder items. Each menu item can be linked to various types of content, including applications, workspaces, or external URLs. 

Setting Permissions: Administrators can configure access permissions for each menu item, ensuring that users only view content relevant to their specific roles and access levels.

The out-of-the-box "Home" section in the Site Menu is available by default but if a different landing page is preferred, the "Home" section can be disabled and replaced with other content, such as a custom workpages created in the site menu content.

 

 

Understanding the different Menu Item types

To create and effective and  user-friendly Site Menu, there are different types of menu a user can choose from, the most important being Custom, Workspace, Application, and Predefined. These items play significant roles in tailoring the navigation experience to meet the specific needs of different user groups and provide flexibility to the diverse business needs of our customers. Here's a detailed look at each of these menu item types:

Custom

Description: Custom menu items provide maximum flexibility, allowing administrators to link to a wide range of content both within and outside of the SAP Build Work Zone environment. These items can direct users to custom URLs or specific workpages stored within the site menu content.

Best use case: integrating unique resources like company intranet workpages, custom-built applications, or specialized informational workpages that don't fit into standard categories like applications or workspaces.

Workspaces

Description: Workspace menu items link directly to specific workspaces within SAP Build Work Zone. Workspaces are collaborative areas that contain various applications, information, and tools designed to support specific business processes, projects, or team activities.

Best use case: providing quick access to collaborative environments, helping users navigate to project areas, departmental resources, or group-specific workspaces. These type enhances collaboration and productivity by centralizing access to related resources.

Application

Description: Application menu items link directly to individual applications within SAP Build Work Zone, including standard SAP apps, custom-developed solutions, or third-party applications integrated into the platform.

Best use case: quick, easy access to tools needed for daily tasks. Application items simplify the UX by reducing the steps required to launch frequently used applications, improving overall efficiency.

Predefined Menu Items

Description: Predefined menu items are system-generated links that provide access to commonly used SAP Build Work Zone features or services. These items, like the Recommendation feature or the cross-workspace Knowledge Base, are automatically included in the Site Menu for consistent access to key functionalities.

Best use case: ensuring all users have standardized access to core features such as the Knowledge Base or workspace dropdowns. They maintain a consistent navigation structure, making it easier for users to find essential services across the platform.

 

Transporting Site Menu Configurations

Transporting Site Menu configurations is essential for maintaining consistency across different environments (for example, from a development to a production environment). This process involves:

Exporting the Configuration: Utilize the transport functionality within the Site Manager to export the current Site Menu configuration. This generates a file containing all menu settings and structures.

You have two options: either export all content items from the Menu Content screen or export only the content referenced by the menu. This choice is determined by checking or unchecking the corresponding checkbox in the prompted popup.

Importing to the Target System: In the destination system, use the Site Menu configuration screen to import the file. This applies the exported menu settings to the target system, ensuring a seamless  user experience across environments.

  

 

Best Practices for Site Menu Management:

  • Keep It Simple: Aim for a Site Menu structure that is easy to understand and navigate. Overcomplicating the menu can make it harder for users to find what they need.
  • Regularly Review and Update: As the needs of your organization change, so should your Site Menu structure and content to ensure it remains relevant and useful.
  • Engage Users for Feedback: Users feedback is invaluable to understand their real needs and preferences and optimize the Site Menu.

 

If you need additional information on workspaces, you can access the SAP Help Portal:

Overview of site menu

If you have more questions on SAP Build Work Zone, visit our FAQ page on SAP Community.

More Information on new Features and Functionalities:

Check out the roadmap for standard & advanced edition.

entry.title: ECATT for SU10 does not work in background processing
entry.content:
Dear all!

My ECATT for TCD SU10 runs in foreground without any troubles but by execution in a scheduled background job I get the error message "No batch input data for dynpro SAPMSSY0 0120".

Can someone tell me how I can solve?

Tx and regards,

Mario

entry.title: Re: writing in same table without locking it
entry.content:
I don't know whether it is worthwhile to discuss AI recommendations – apparently you can try those suggestions with a "staging table".

Personally, I'd favour a solution without separate "helper tables", where each transaction

  • either just updates a single row (so using many short transactions to prevent race conditions),
  • or "marks" those rows it is about to update (by explicitly blocking those against other writers, or by updating a "flag" on that row saying "I'm about to doing more work here"). Another transaction would then notice that it cannot block these rows itself, would skip over them and could "mark" its own set of rows.

entry.title: SAP Advanced Financial Closing: Leveraging Workflows to Extend Entity Closes
entry.content:
Background

You are implementing or already using SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC). As part of automating the month-end closing process, you want to extend your entity close in a way SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) doesn't support out of the box. With the latest incremental release of SAP Advanced Financial Closing in November 2024, we allow to integrate workflows, reflecting processes defined in SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), with SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC). This also allows you to include SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) bots into your entity close process by calling them within a workflow.

With that you can extend your entity close process e.g. by 

  • two step approval workflows,
  • approval workflows with dynamic determination of the approver,
  • robotic automation by calling a bot within the SBPA process.

Getting Started: A Step-By-Step Guide

Assuming that you have a license for SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), you can achieve the integration of workflows as follows:

1. Set up SAP Build Process Automation Instance

Where: BTP Cockpit

First, if not yet done, you need to set up an SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) instance. 

Note: Currently, the instances of SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) and SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) are required to be in the same BTP sub-account.

2. Connect AFC to SAP Build Process Automation Instance

Where: SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Specify Communication Systems"

We now connect SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) to SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC). For this, the following steps are required:

  1. Create a new communication system of type SAP Build Process Automation System.

  2. Click "Create". Then, the respective communication system is available.

Note:

  • The current status of the SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) instance can tracked in the "Monitor Communication Systems" app.
  • Once created, connection check and navigation to SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) is possible by using the "Monitor Communication Systems" app.
  • Value helps and actions in SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) related to SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) are visible only if a system of type SAP Build Process Automation System was maintained.
  • Columns in SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) related to SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) are by default hidden but available in the respective table settings.

3. Defining Workflows in SAP Build Process Automation

Where: SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)

A thorough documentation on SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), and in particular how to create and maintain workflows, can be found on SAP Help Portal: SAP Build Process Automation.

As a result, you have created a project in SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) containing processes that are reflected as workflows in SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC). 

4. Include Workflows in AFC Templates

Where: SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Manage Closing Task Lists"

It's now possible to include tasks of type "Workflow" in templates and task lists as follows:

  • Create a task from workflow via action "Create Task from Workflow".
  • Select one of the workflows via value help. 
  • Click "Create".

  • The result is a new task of type "Workflow" with basically the same attributes (and thus behavior / functionality) as for ABAP jobs.
    Additional attributes: External Job ID (which is the workflow ID)
    Attributes not applicable to workflows: Remote Processing User

Note:

Value Help

  • All workflows maintained in the connected SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) system are shown here, there's no (hard-coded) filtering.
  • The available workflows are directly retrieved from processes defined in the connected SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) system.
  • There is no duplicate persistence in SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC).

Parameters

  • A predefined set of parameters is mapped automatically (see SAP Help Portal: Integration of Tasks of Type Workflow).
  • Other parameters can be maintained manually.
  • Arrays are not yet supported (planned to be improved in one of the next releases).
  • Relative dates (rules) are not yet supported (planned to be improved in one of the next releases).
  • Dates are converted into supported type (DATE).

5. Start Workflow

Where: SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Process Closing Tasks"

The workflow added to the template (and thus to generated task lists), can then be started as follows:

  1. Schedule the workflow.

  2. Check the scheduling details.

Note: Workflows behave similar to ABAP jobs regarding

  • planned start / duration,
  • start mode,
  • executions (split per company code), and
  • processors (which are also mandatory).

Workflows behave different to ABAP jobs:

  • Only SENT parameters are available as sent & used parameters are always the same for workflows.
  • "Remote Processing User" is not supported.

6. Processing Workflows

Where: SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)

A thorough documentation on SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), and in particular how to create and maintain workflows, can be found on SAP Help Portal: SAP Build Process Automation

Note: In order to get (steps of) a workflow in the SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) inbox, this step needs to be assigned to the respective SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) user.

7. Check Workflow Results

Where: SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Process Closing Tasks"

As soon as the workflow was finished, this reflects in the status of the respective task:

  • Status
  • Output parameters
  • Logs

Note:

Status

Currently, we only show the "technical status" of the workflow process:

  • Completed without errors (workflow runs through)
  • Failed (workflow run fails)

Handover of business status planned in one of the next releases.

Retry Mechanism

  • It's possible to retry technically failed workflows on SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) side. This might change the status of the task from "Failed" to "Completed w/o Errors" even though the task was already started again on SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) side.
  • The SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) log of the task might be extended (by the messages from the retry). 

Output Parameters

  • Output parameters handed back via JSON (chunked if required)
  • Planned to be improved in one of the next releases.

8. Check Reporting

Where:  SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Closing Task Completion"

The workflows can of course also be checked in the reporting apps (filter e.g. for task type "Workflow").

Summary

Using SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) workflows, you can extend your entity close in a way SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) doesn't support out of the box.

For more information on SAP Advanced Financial Closing, check out the following links:

    • SAP Community Page
    • SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Roadmap Explorer
    • SAP.com Page

Follow us via #AdvancedFinancialClosing, SAP Advanced Financial Closing, or myself via @ThomasLuecking.

Disclaimer

Please note that SAP Advanced Financial Closing gets frequent updates and enhancements. Therefore, the current features and functions may not be exactly as described here. For the latest information, you can refer to the product documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

Please note that the information given here or any related document and SAP’s strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. The information in this document is not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver the functionality described here or any related material or code. Instead, the information is for informational purposes only. It may not be incorporated into a contract. SAP assumes no responsibility for errors or omissions in this document, except if such damages were caused by SAP’s intentional or gross negligence.

Update Q4/2023: The official product name of AFC has been simplified from SAP S/4HANA Cloud for advanced financial closing to SAP Advanced Financial Closing. This doesn’t affect neither the strategy nor its functionalities, it is just a new product name.

entry.title: Different Storage Bin in MMBE and LS24
entry.content:
Hello,

There is different Storage Bin in MMBE and LS24.  The storage bin showing in MMBE is correct.  Any idea how the storage bin has changed in LS24 (entered by user? in which tcode?).  How to correct this.

Thank you,

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content:
As to question 1,  users who you want to receive forms for any process must be given the role collection ProcessAutomationParticipant.

Users should be set up in the custom IDP (though I think this is not mandatory for Process Automation) and you should provide the role collections in the correct IDP.

When you "get the users from IDP", what do you mean?

entry.title: Re: Create SAP S/4HANA External GL Account Hierarchy within SAP Datasphere using standard CDS Views
entry.content:
Hello @Sefan_Linders 

Would you be so kind, maybe to give a tip what could be the root cause on my issue above?

Many thanks in advance!

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows in 2024
entry.content:
Hi Andreas,

Thanks for the information about when the steampunk maintenance windows occur. My client is a global organization with a multi-regional deployment. I cannot find any articles on the best strategy on how to handle this period. I thought about using a load balancer to re-direct any incoming calls to, for example, to a RAP service. However looking at these maintenance periods,  there are times when even globally not one of the regions is actually available. I think the 4 periods per year is perhaps manageable through various strategies, but the fact that it is possible it could be down every weekend that concerns me. Could you offer some advice please.

regards

James M.

entry.title: XML Properties to Json format
entry.content:
Hello everyone , i face the following issue:

I have properties that are in XML format :

 because the body is in xml. I want to take all the properties and make an JSON Log attachment file (errorProperties.json) that have them transformed in json format. The issue is that there are properties in XML . I tried to convert them via Groovy script , using base64->string but it doesnt work. Do you have any ideas?

entry.title: Re: ADS Configuration
entry.content:
Hi Samar,

Please let me know how to use this ADS rfc to make connection and get the ADOBE TEMPLATE.

Navin K.

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP...
entry.content:
As for question 2, you are saying that you want to give to allow more people to give the ProcessAutomationParticipant role collection to users. To give role collections to people you must have some admin rights on the BTP subaccount level. You may have to give subaccount admin rights, which are pretty powerful rights.

I will ask if there is another way.

entry.title: Re: Extension Project in Business Application Studio ?
entry.content:
Hello @OliverGraeff,

We are developing a custom SAPUI5 app that we integrate with our My Inbox. The My Inbox extension that we created is an Adaptation Project, done in Business Application Studio. It was successfully deployed and our My Inbox adaptation app is able to load our custom UI5, but we are having problems with the custom approval that we trigger in the custom app using the scaffolding, since the My Inbox is not using scaffolding anymore.

My question is:

  1. Is it correct that we created an Adaptation Project for the My Inbox or should we have created an Extension Project instead? Our UI5 Version of the deployed My Inbox standard app is 1.120.11.
  2. What is the difference between the Adaptation Project and Extension Project? When should we use Adaptation Project and when should we use Extension Project?
  3. If it should be an Extension Project, how do we do this in Business Application Studio? I have only seen how to create an Adaptation Project in BAS, but not an Extension Project.
  4. If we have implemented correctly (using the Adaptation Project), is there a way to customized the approval of the requests without using scaffolding? 

Appreciate your help on this. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to assign Users as Approver and Requester in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: Also see https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/authorizations

entry.title: Re: SAP Event Mesh | End To End Tutorial with Cloud Platform Integration
entry.content:
Hello, please can you clarify exactly what method you are using to trigger the hana to CPI step one HTTPS sender.      I am trying to get to grips with event mesh from hana (onprem) to cpi.   I was assuming that sap business accelerator hub/event objects were what we need to use.   Though what you have appears to be a normal api.   The event objects appear to be limited (for my needs), so hopefully I can use other apis.  I just need to know how to trigger the sender. @BarisBuyuktanir talks about the netweaver event enablement package, so perhaps this is my answer, and I need to talk to my basis team.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to setup Model View OData
entry.content:
Hi @juancortes,

whenever you require some help, I suggest you to create a proper question with the Q&A tool of the SAP Community.

Just to be sure, did you try clicking on the little pencil close to the "Fields" area?
There you should be able to check fields and related metadata retrieved by the OData source, and then map them to fields that you "declared" in you environment.

Hope it helps

entry.title: Re: Equipment Functional Location Search Not Working
entry.content: Issue resolved with this note https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2699982

entry.title: Business rule to trigger a custom email template based on Job info attributes
entry.content:
Hi experts!

I am trying to configure a rule which triggers a workflow that is designed to send the manager of an individual a custom email template.

The rule looks at the probationary end date and if today is -28 days from it, it triggers the workflow.

Since the removal of the basic rule type, i cant seem to find the right scenario to base the rule on.

I have tried the generate alerts rule type, and whilst that does trigger the workflow and trigger an alert, it does not send the custom email template assigned to the workflow at all.

attached are snips of the rule and the workflow currently.

Thanks!email

entry.title: Re: PCE Brownfield migration procedure.
entry.content:
Hi,

  1. The Migration Server is part of RISE PCE, to be used for the duration of the migration project.
  2. The Migration Server is an unmanaged Linux IaaS server.
  3. Follow the Partner Led Migrations - ECS supported Migration approaches document you have referenced, for your chosen approach. Using export/import, SUM DMO with system move, backup/restore. Consider running SWPM on the migration server with the HANA backup.

Regards, Phillip

entry.title: Ad-Hoc Product WT via RF not working (/SCWM/RF_DE259)
entry.content:
Hello,

I have issues creating product warehouse tasks to move stock from 1 HU to another using /SCWM/RFUI. I receive error message "/SCWM-RF_DE259 - Quantity must be greater than zero". The stock is available and entered accordingly and there are no open warehouse tasks for this stock so I don't understand this message.

Screenshots attached.

Thanks in advance,

Jonas

entry.title: Re: Steps to import Private Key (PPK) in SAP PI/PO for key based authentication
entry.content: Note: If you use an old version SAP PO you need to use OpenSSL 1.1 unsupported version to create the P12 Key file.

entry.title: Re: Can we create the new depreciation key in sap in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi,

You cannot create any new depreciation keys and you can also not change any existing depreciation keys.

Please check the following SAP Help Documentation,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/a19cc2531bb9b44ce10000000a174cb4.html#:~:text=You%20cannot%20create%20any%20new%20depreciation%20keys%20and%20you%20can%20also%20not%20change%20any%20existing%20depreciation%20keys.

Kind Regards,

Lucy

entry.title: Re: Time to delivery Application Server for Linux
entry.content:
Hi,

8018042 - 14 working days, assuming this is a CR.

Please consult the Provisioning Timelines document on the PCA Wiki for this information.

Regards, Phillip

entry.title: Extend Validity of E-Waybill
entry.content:
How to extend the validity of E-Waybill?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Extend Validity of E-Waybill
entry.content:
The validity of EWB can be extended between 8 hours before expiry time and 8 hours after expiry time. The same is mentioned in the Extend Validity link from NIC.

Refer section 10.7 from the attachment "eWay_Bill_Installation_and_Configurations.pdf" from the note ##2631687 - SAP solution for e-Way Bill India

entry.title: Re: MB5B negative opening period stock altough no materia...
entry.content:
Hi 

To analyze MB5B, share screen shot with material document and document number cloumn in the output screen.

 

Also check initial screen and share screen shot

Then we can analyze it again. 

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: Adaptation Projects – it’s time for a new way to extend your Fiori Elements applications
entry.content:
Hello @Margot @hristotsolev 

We are developing a custom SAPUI5 app that we integrate with our My Inbox. The My Inbox extension that we created is an Adaptation Project, done in Business Application Studio. It was successfully deployed and our My Inbox adaptation app is able to load our custom UI5, but we are having problems with the custom approval that we trigger in the custom app using the scaffolding, since the My Inbox is not using scaffolding anymore.

My question is:

  1. Is it correct that we created an Adaptation Project for the My Inbox or should we have created an Extension Project instead? Our UI5 Version of the deployed My Inbox standard app is 1.120.11.
  2. What is the difference between the Adaptation Project and Extension Project? When should we use Adaptation Project and when should we use Extension Project?
  3. If it should be an Extension Project, how do we do this in Business Application Studio? I have only seen how to create an Adaptation Project in BAS, but not an Extension Project.
  4. If we have implemented correctly (using the Adaptation Project), is there a way to customized the approval of the requests without using scaffolding? 

Appreciate your help on this. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: SAP SDK change item group without changing item master data
entry.content:
In B1, if an user changes the item group, he get's a message "Do you want to update the item information (including UoM group)according to the selected item group?" and the user can answer No and B1 will not copy the data that is in the item group to the item master data. 

I want the same but using SDK. I have a program that uploads data from a XML file and I don't want to update the MRP data that is in the item master data with the data that is in the item group.

Basically I want to answer this  "Do you want to update the item information (including UoM group)according to the selected item group?" with a No but in SDK.

How can I do it?

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Different Storage Bin in MMBE and LS24
entry.content:
Hi 

In MMBE we dont have storage bin. It is for IM managed stock report. 

In LS24 It shows storage type T01 and storage location 4004.  in both report stock quantity is 1. 

Could you check LS26 and share screen shot 

then we will check further

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: EMMA / BPEM : automatic cases creation
entry.content:
Hi Ashwathi,

To use your own exceptions there are some requirements (see screen shot below):

  1. You need to check if EL18 is available in the customizing setting Enhanced Message management -> Specifications for Customer-Defined Business Processes and Messages -> Define Customer Transactions for Message Management. 
    If not, add it.
  2. You need to add your exception to the BPEM customizing setting and provide the data contained in the parameters of the exception in Enhanced Message management -> Specifications for Customer-Defined Business Processes and Messages -> Define Business Objects for Customer-Defined Messages

Once done, you can run EL18 again so that it produces the SLG1 entry.

Identify the EL18 transaction you just ran in EMMAJL and prepare the job found, check if your exception is in the job log and see if it is identified as an error. If so, after preparation, create cases and the case should appear. If the exception is not there, make sure your coding where the exception is written, is using the same BP Code as the EL18 transaction.

Hope this helps, otherwise, let me know.

 

Best Regards,

Hans Kardol

entry.title: Re: Absence Quota Generation - PT60
entry.content:
Hello Sai,

The system will look for a current record that is valid on that day, so if the second accrual is within the 30 days of the 1st, it will not create a new record, which sounds like what you are seeing. I believe you can resolve this by changing the way you create the dates on the records. You can set the validity date to be the date of the accrual but set the deduction date to be 30 days out. So, for your accrual on 1/11/2024, the validity dates would be 1/11/2024 - 1/11/2024 but the deduction dates would be 1/11/2024 - 2/10/2024. Then, if you have another overtime/accrual on 2/1/2024, I believe it will create a new record based on the validity dates, but the deduction dates still allow all of the hours to be used.

The downside to this, in my opinion, is visibility, so you would want to train your users to look at a range of dates when viewing IT2006, or maybe other reports might be affected as well. It has been a while since I have set these up, but I believe this should work for you situation.

Happy configuring,

Cindy

entry.title: Dashboard Linking and Filtering Through Table Hyperlinks in SAC.
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m working on a project where we need to link two dashboards within SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) that both use the same BW live model. We’re attempting to use table hyperlinks to connect the dashboards, but we’re running into issues with both the Link to Story and Link to External ID options, as neither seem to work as expected.

Both dashboards utilize prompts for filtering, and we need to ensure that the selected filter values (from one dashboard) are passed correctly to the other. Has anyone encountered a similar challenge or found a solution for linking and filtering dashboards through table hyperlinks?

I’d really appreciate any insights or suggestions! 

Thank you in advance for your time and help. @William_Yu

entry.title: Re: Error in learning journey content / designing asset a...
entry.content: Thanks for your help!

entry.title: Preview Your App
entry.content:
Olá, estou tendo problemas para testar meu aplicativo no celular. Quando tento, ele pede um código PIN no aplicativo do celular que coloco e dá com sessão expirada.

Eu moro no Brasil.

entry.title: Re: Different Storage Bin in MMBE and LS24
entry.content: Thank you Rashid,

entry.title: How to use initial HTTP request (not from HTTP client)
entry.content:
Hi there,

I want to send a newly created HTTP request based on if_http_request or cl_http_request without using the request which is stored in the HTTP client.

I guess the default way to send a HTTP request looks kind of like this:

 

DATA(client) = cl_http_client=>create( ).
" Prepare request
client->request->set_method( ... ).
client->request->set_header_field( ... ).
...

client->send( ... ).

 

By debugging, this way is working, not least because the request object stored in the client contains a member attribute named m_c_msg that is initialized.

I would like to decouple the creation and initialization of the HTTP request by underlying the HTTP client:

 

DATA(client) = cl_http_client=>create( ).

" Prepare request
DATA(request) = NEW cl_http_request( ).
request->set_method( ... ).
request->set_header_field( ... ).
...

client->request = request.
client->send( ... ).

 

In my way the request seems not to be initialized correctly and therefore the sending fails.

Can anyone tell me if it is possible to implement my prefered solution and give me some hints?

BR

entry.title: Handle domain-driven authorization in a Node.js CAP app that uses PostgreSQL as database
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I'm developing a CAP application in Node.js (with TypeScript) that needs to check for authorizations on a domain level via associations navigation. If, for example, I have a Foo entity that has an association to Bar entity that associates itself to a Third entity that has a scalar field Field. I want to check if that Field has a specific value (maybe compared to one of the $user attributes that come from the authentication, for the sake of this example) and, if so, I would serve only the data related to a specific role that respect the given conditions.
So far so good, I can leverage the domain-driven authorizations and just navigate through the entity associations to check the value I want:

 

annotate DummyService.Foo with @(
    restrict    : [{
        grant: 'READ',
        to   : 'user',
        where: 'bar.third.field = $user.field'
    }]
);

 

But this didn't seem to work at all, I never got to have any records back. After a lot of trial and error I dug in the CAP documentation and I found out that associations navigation is not (yet, hopefully) possible outside of a SAP HANA context.

I'm using PostgreSQL so, if I didn't misunderstand that warning, I guess I have to handle it by myself in the custom handlers.

Fine, I have set up a class for the DummyService to handle the on.before event for reading Foo records. The idea is to inject the privilege check into the where clause of the req.query.SELECT object to obtain something on the line of:

 

req.query.SELECT.where = [{<query filters>}, "and", {ref: ["bar", "third", "field"]}, "=", {val: req.user.attr.field}];

 

The problem is that I can't figure out how to inject data into the where variable, since TypeScript complains about type compatibility with whatever I try to push into the conditions array.
At runtime, the where object is an Array of objects so it wouldn't be difficult at all to push something into it, but at design time stuff is a bit different. In fact, the where object adheres to the following type:

 

Partial<string & SELECT_2 & ref & val & xpr & function_call>'

 

This is what I have tried to do so far:

 

import cds from "@sap/cds";

export default class DummyService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  init() {
    this.before("READ", "Foo", (req) => {
      req.query.SELECT?.where?.push("and");
      req.query.SELECT?.where?.push({xpr: [{ref: ["bar", "third", "field"]}, "=", {val: req.user.attr.field}]});
    });

    return super.init();
  }
}

 

And the specific error is:
Argument of type '{ xpr: (string | { ref: string[]; } | { val: string; })[]; }' is not assignable to parameter of type 'Partial<string & SELECT_2 & ref & val & xpr & function_call>'.

The types returned by 'valueOf()' are incompatible between these types.
Type 'Object' is not assignable to type 'string'.

Has someone ever had to manage this kind of scenario? Is there an actual way to do this?

Thanks in advance for any help,
Federico

entry.title: Re: Different Storage Bin in MMBE and LS24
entry.content: Thank you Rashid, We know that MMBE stock is based on SLoc. But in the above MMBE pic it is showing storage bin name 'Gas WH' (from Material Master). The LS26 screenshot is shown below.

entry.title: Incorrect download link for 32 bit Crystal for visual studio
entry.content: The link for the 32-bit download leads to the 64 bit for VS 2022 and above: https://origin-az.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/file/0020000000638712024

entry.title: Re: UI5ers Buzz #55: Adaptation Project - your one stop tool for extending SAPUI5 Applications
entry.content:
Hello @hristotsolev 

We are developing a custom SAPUI5 app that we integrate with our My Inbox. The My Inbox extension that we created is an Adaptation Project, done in Business Application Studio. It was successfully deployed and our My Inbox adaptation app is able to load our custom UI5, but we are having problems with the custom approval that we trigger in the custom app using the scaffolding, since the My Inbox is not using scaffolding anymore.

My question is:

  1. Is it correct that we created an Adaptation Project for the My Inbox or should we have created an Extension Project instead? Our UI5 Version of the deployed My Inbox standard app is 1.120.11.
  2. What is the difference between the Adaptation Project and Extension Project? When should we use Adaptation Project and when should we use Extension Project?
  3. If it should be an Extension Project, how do we do this in Business Application Studio? I have only seen how to create an Adaptation Project in BAS, but not an Extension Project.
  4. If we have implemented correctly (using the Adaptation Project), is there a way to customized the approval of the requests without using scaffolding? 

Appreciate your help on this. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Different Storage Bin in MMBE and LS24
entry.content:
As I said that MMBE will show report on basis storage IM. in material master you maintain bin in Plant and storage location views by using MM01. see thread for MMBE

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/storage-bin-in-mmbe/qaq-p/6664169

 

LS24 will show report on warehouse mangement bin. that is assigned in storage type. it will be different

This bin is created by LS01N in WM 

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: writing in same table without locking it
entry.content:
Here is an example how I can regenerate the error which I am getting in production:

create or replace table MyInventary (ProductName varchar(10) primary key, Quantity int);
insert into MyInventary values ('Mouse', 10), ('Keyboard', 20), ('Ram', 100), ('HDD', 15);
create or replace procedure ReceiveSinglePiecesShipping(ProductList long varchar)
begin
for ShippedProducts as mycursor cursor for select row_value as ProductInCursor from sa_split_list(ProductList) do
waitfor delay ('00:00:01');
update MyInventary set Quantity = Quantity - 1 where ProductName = trim(ProductInCursor) ;
end for;
end;

 

And then I call the procedure from two separate connections:
Connection1: call ReceiveSinglePiecesShipping('Mouse,Keyboard,RAM,Mouse');commit;
Connection2: call ReceiveSinglePiecesShipping('HDD, RAM, Mouse,Keyboard,RAM');commit;

I get a deadlock with the second connection and it is rollbacked.

entry.title: SAP EWM Podcast - Episode 02 - Integrated or Decentralized Warehouse Management with SAP EWM
entry.content:
Welcome to our blog post for the SAP EWM Podcast Episode 02 – Integrated or Decentralized Warehouse Management with SAP EWM - What Should I Do?

You can listen to the podcast episode in English and in German on Spotify, Amazon Music, Apple Podcasts or Sway! Enjoy!

Embedded vs. decentralized EWM 

Embedded EWM is part of SAP S/4HANA and can only be used with an existing S/4 installation. It is the EWM that is fully integrated into SAP S/4HANA  and ist is used in the same system like other logistic applications MM, SD, LE etc.. There are benefits of an embedded EWM for example here is no master data distribution from the ERP module and embedded EWM supports synchronous postings. 

Decentralized EWM means a separate installation that is supplied with the necessary data via native SAP to SAP interfaces, but on the other hand can work completely autonomously.

SAP EWM Deployment Options

 

Decision Criteria 

There are functional, operational, and strategic aspects that should be considered when you have to take a decision. 

Decision criteria embedded vs. decentral EWM

1) Functional decision criteria

The first thing to consider when making this decision is the functional differences between the two options: To make an example: If you want to connect several ERP clients or ERP systems to an EWM, you cannot use an embedded EWM for this but only a decentralized EWM. Beyond that capability there are rather soft functional criteria such as synchronous production integration in embedded EWM, which of course provides real added value especially for production-related warehouses. Soft criteria however means also that the processes are  supported in embedded EWM as well as in decentralized EWM. Often the differences are rather technical - from an end-user perspective there is normally no differences. 

The functional differences are described for each release in the corresponding release notes. There is also an PDF attachment to the notes which shows the differences in transparent table format: 

Excerpt from the functional comparison for SAP EWM on S/4HANA 2023

 

SAP Note 3376455 - Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS00, FPS01 and FPS02
SAP Note 3376453 - SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS00, FPS01 and FPS02: Release information and restrictions for EWM in SAP S/4HANA

2) Operational decision criteria

Operational decision criteria assess the influence of a deployment on the operation of the solution. The independence of warehouse management from the ERP system for example is one of the major operational aspects. Independence can mean different things: For example the fact that if there is a downtime in the ERP, the warehouse can continue to work for the time being. Purchase orders that have already been distributed can be received and distributed outbound deliveries can be picked and shipped. The standard integration of both systems ensures that everything is posted correctly after the connection is restored.

This is ensured by the native SAP to SAP integration between EWM and ERP which uses qRFCs (queued remote function calls) for the exchange of transactional data which offer an error-tolerant integration close to real time. This native integration supports for example the distribution of purchase orders or late changes of customer orders and outbound deliveries on ERP side with automatic updates on EWM side.  

Another operational aspect is the performance and latency of the EWM systems. It can make sense or it can be necessary to operate EWM system close to the  warehouses. This does not mean that the systems have to be at the warehouse location, this is in general not necessary. But it could be required that the data center of a hypersaler is at least in the same region than the warehouse for example in Europe, Americas or in Asia. In this case a decentralized EWM is necessary if there is a single ERP system hosted for example in Europe. 

A decentralized EWM also gives more independence when it comes to maintenance and release cycles. Embedded EWM implementations must be upgraded together with the SAP S/4HANA ERP system. This can lead to bottlenecks in project and test capacities or you simply don't want to take the risk of a joint upgrade for all divisions and all warehouses.

Technical dependency can also play a role. In a decentralized EWM landscape customers do not have to coordinate with the entire SAP S/4HANA system when importing process changes. This can definitely increase the flexibility of logistics and IT departments. And finally customers have to check whether their SAP S/4HANA system has sufficient resources to operate the entire warehouse management - it's about HANA memory, application servers and so on. 

There are technical capabilities for 'embedded' EWM to get the technical infrastructure level more similar to the 'decentralized' EWM in which a whole ABAP system is dedicated for EWM for example by the placement of the EWM table group to a dedicated HANA DB scale-out node or by using dedicated application servers only for EWM users. Please check the following SAP notes for more details: 

1423066 - Optimization of the performance in EWM
2950948 - EWM table group for placement on HANA DB scale-out node

To clarify system performance aspect a greenfield system sizing should also be made as part of the deployment decision. Especially for high-volume and high performance warehouses with a lot of warehouse automation the sizing is a decisive point. The topic of automation is also always an important point: With EWM the whole material flow can be control with the integrated material flow system (EWM-MFS). External material flow systems are no longer required and EWM communicates directly with the PLCs.

Warehouse automation is a strong argument for the decentralized approach - but not one that excludes the embedded EWM option. You really have to check the specific requirements. There are SAP EWM customers who control their automated warehouses directly with embedded EWM. This can also work without any problems. A sizing you is highly recommended when automation is used. 

Sizing for SAP Extended Warehouse Management

 Please check the following links to get more information about sizing for EWM: 

SAP Sizing - HANA based Quicksizer 
SAP Note 1896197 - Sizing of Extended Warehouse Management - Best Practices

2) Strategic decision criteria

The central question when it comes to strategic decision criteria is “how will my business and my logistics develop in the future?". Answers to questions like "Do we expect strong growth", "do we have an automation strategy" or "will we have many new logistics sites" influence the decision and can rather speak for a decentralized approach - to ensure independence and performance. 

It is also conceivable to start the S/4HANA journey with a decentralized EWM on S/4HANA: We call this "logistics first approach". Customers can thus reduce the complexity of a transformation, as they only have to deal with warehouse logistics in the first step.  Later the other SAP modules follow. The integration of the decentralized EWM on S/4HANA is then only be switched from ECC to S/4HANA. Thanks to the same technical integration the complexity of switching is low and the effort is manageable.

Logistics first approach with SAP EWM

Hybrid approaches are also possible and on the roadmap of many SAP customers. This means that embedded EWM is for example used for smaller sales warehouses or production-related warehouses and decentralized EWM is used for the large distribution centers. The big advantage is that customers can rely on one single solution for their whole intralogistics - in production and distribution - since the source code and the application is the same. Even re-use of customer specific EWM extensions is in a hybrid landscape possible. 

Summary  

The answer to the question "embedded or decentralized EWM - what should I do?" depends on many aspects. In addition it is not simply possible to switch between the deployment options just by configuration. It is possible due to the fact that there is only one EWM allowing both options but the switch is always a migration project. In order to come to a good decision, it is required that customers do an individual assessment of the criteria mentioned above. Assessment tools like a utility analysis, decision trees other methods can support this process.

Helpful Links: 

SAP Note 1606493 - SAP EWM Deployment Options Best Practices

Please visit our SAP EWM Podcast Landing Page with all Episodes, Blog Posts and Podcast Notes!

entry.title: Re: Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to get your answer faster!

entry.title: Admin Proxy in the New Learning Experience
entry.content: The Admin Proxy in the New Learning Experience now allows the Admin to Start Course on behalf of the user.  Is that expected behaviour?  In the legacy version the Admin wasn't able to see "START COURSE" next to the course.  They should not be able to complete a course on behalf of the user.

entry.title: Re: Merge dimension using left of string
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Merge dimension using left of string
entry.content: Hi James - thank you for your response. I am not able to merge a variable with a dimension. Is thought this was a restriction in BO

Dear All,

Is it possible to use NSE Native Storage Extension in an S/4HANA RISE environment?

If yes, who will be in charge to configure it, partner or SAP, and what cautions we need to take care regarding license?

 

Thank you.

M.N.

entry.title: Re: OPEN ODS VIEW TO HANDLE 2 MILLING RECORDS
entry.content:
What exactly is the problem? Where are you stuck?

For start, I would suggest to go through these:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/bwplaolap/3361385376.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace -Send shipment status to customer/buyer using easy r
entry.content: You don't need E2A script to pass parameter values. The 'Placeholeder Name' and Value mapping in email rule screen does this.

entry.title: Re: can I use CORRESPONDING # for deep internal tables?
entry.content: @sh4il3sh it should work for internal tables too. Maybe create a sample test code (including local TYPES) and post it here.

entry.title: Re: HIERARCHIN IN OPEN ODS VIEW
entry.content:
ODSV only supports these semantics:

  • Facts
  • Texts
  • Master Data

See the document:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/04030263a0d041309a039fa3ea586720/e7e9e52dbf114a24bfa3a817f7ea3322.html?locale=en-US

If you need hierarchies, you need a characteristic with "Hierarchies" enabled. The hierarchies can be either local (load or create them in your BW) or it can be remote:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/ab216bfb8b2b452a860267b1e40e270f/1dec6d36681e4a0d8806a63cb925b9ad.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Best way to generate Microsoft Excel xlsx from template in ABAP
entry.content:
Hello,

Very good solution, @antonsikidin . Do you have an example of how to export an image to an excel file?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Downpayment request from sales order for US not being cleared by incoming payment
entry.content:
We have an issue with DP request for US where the tax calculation happens via taxjar integration and tax code config in SAP has 0%. The DP request created from sales order billing cannot be cleared with incoming payment with the following error message - Tax entry not possible in this item (Message no. FF724). However, the F2 invoice document can be cleared with incoming payment which means all our downpayments for US remains uncleared. This works fine for non-US company code where system is calculating the tax. 

However, if we try to create a manual FI DP request for US with some tax value and clear the same with incoming payment, this works fine. 

The issue happens only when we try to get it integrated with SD with FAZ doc type for DP req and it is not letting us go ahead in clearing the DP request with payment as in any normal std flow.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting- Anonymize Candidate Profile and Applications
entry.content: What does the candidate see in their Job Applications webpage after anonymisation.

entry.title: Simple diagnostic installation error - Connect to <hostname>: 1129 failed: NIECONN_REFUSED(-10)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Whie installing simple diagnostic agent(SDA) via agent admin UI we are getting above error message.

Please suggest me here.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to use initial HTTP request (not from HTTP client)
entry.content:
When creating the new request by using a system call like in the original SAP code I receive a 401 response.

SYSTEM-CALL ict "#EC CI_SYSTEMCALL
  DID
    ihttp_scid_create_message
  PARAMETERS
    1
    message_pointer
    error.

DATA(request) = NEW cl_http_request( c_msg = message_pointer ).

entry.title: Re: Merge dimension using left of string
entry.content: You can merge variables with dimensions from BI4.2 SP3 onwards

entry.title: Re: Modify the Financial Review booklet
entry.content: Thank you very much for sharing!

entry.title: SAC exception aggregation type is lost after import job execution
entry.content:
Hi all

we have an SAC import model which is updated daily from BW query. Among other data which are loaded, we have some accounts which have exception aggregation type and exception aggregation dimension maintained and it  behaves in stories as expected.

The issue is that since Monday 18th Nov. after the import job runs and loads data to the model the aggregation type gets lost and the model has validation errors. Does anyone else has the same issue?

thank you

entry.title: Re: Anonymise Selected Job Applications in Data Retention Management
entry.content: What does the candidate see in their Job Applications webpage after application anonymisation.

entry.title: Re: Best way to generate Microsoft Excel xlsx from template in ABAP
entry.content: @joanacarolas use the original abap2xlsx and check the samples.

entry.title: Is it possible to get a list of users in SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Hello,

in SAP BTP I use Cloud Identity Services, which has been integrated with AD via Identity Providers. In BTP, in the Security->Users after a successful login, a user is created as a shadow user. Is it possible to use some API to get a list of shadow users? Or get a list of users directly from AD?

Thank you for any help

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @OlgaDolinskaja : For Business Suite on HANA(SoH) migration, will any of the project type support creation of deletion transport request to remove obsolete custom codes in ERP during SoH migration? I am using Custom code migration app in BTP.

I tried Custom code analysis project, but I dont find the option to create a deletion transport request.

If custom code migration app will not work for Suite on HANA migration, is there any plans to support in future as part of custom code analysis project type or a different project type?

Thanks for your response!!

entry.title: Re: Can I create Adaptation Project from my custom Fiori...
entry.content:
Hi @beampakorn.

With a custom ALP app what you need to do is create an extension controller or fragment manually and add your logic. You can't use Adaptation Project which is for standard SAP apps.

Take a look at this example here. 

You can right-click on the webapps folder of your app and try Guided Development options. But I didn't find one that would create an extension controller for an ALP app (there is one for List Report). So what I ended up doing was define a controller extension in my custom ALP app:

Then created a controller file manually in a new folder in webapps > ext > controller:

When I did a build it actually crated the Change file for me (you can actually create change files manually, but not recommended as far as I understand). I'm in an on-premise scenario but it should work the same way in the cloud. Let me know how it works out for you.

Ramin.

entry.title: Re: customer Credit group is not automatically moving fro...
entry.content:
Have you checked the assignment of the credit control/sales area in Customizing (OVFL)?

  • You mentioned display transactions, not change transactions?

entry.title: Re: SOIN -EXCEL data Binary conversion issue - S050 method - PROCESS_INBOUND - cont_hex[]
entry.content: Light XLSX reader: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/quick-n-dirty-solution-for-parsing-xlsx-files-on-the-go/ba-p/13463084

entry.title: S/4 HANA Product Compliance, SDS Shipment goes to "Completed: Shipment Not Required"
entry.content:
Hi All, I am using S/4 HANA Cloud Product Compliance and working on setting up SDS shipment.

When using manual SDS shipment, I am able to select an available and send to an email. In the "SDS Shipment Monitoring" app I am able to see the SDS Shipment. I notice three issues I need some help and guidance.

1. The shipment of the SDS says "Completed: Shipment Not Required". I looked at the help and it says the status refers to SDS shipments for customer who had already received the SDS. But this is the first time I triggered the SDS. Is there a way to force send the SDS? 

2. The coversheet PDF is getting generated as attachment. But the output generation is in "In Preparation" status. Is there any general job or program needs to be done to send the SDS email?

entry.title: Re: How do you want to find out about SAP BTP news and innovations? We want to know!
entry.content:
Hi @cecihuergo 

All formats are great, they are very valuable.

I prefer YouTube videos for practical reasons and especially for the language issue, when I am very interested in a content I use the subtitles that it generates automatically, that is, if I am not mistaken, the other alternatives do not offer them.

However, I repeat, I love all the formats they use and I take this opportunity to thank them for all the knowledge they share day by day, I hope that in 2025 they will do the same or better and we will continue enjoying the content.

Best regards,

Erick

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2023 and Concur-Integration
entry.content:
Dear all,

we are moving from SAP ECC to S/4 HANA 2023 (brownfield conversion) and we are using Concur.

The installed Add-Ons are CTE_FND, CTE_FIN and CTE_HCM. SAP ERP-TO-CONCUR INTEGRATION Release 1.0 SP 21.

Now when preparing the S/4 HANA system for the migration, do we have to deinstall the Add-Ons or do they have an integrated deinstallation routine? I know that the Add-Ons are not required, but in the Maintenance Planner we need this information.

Best Regards,

Afzal

entry.title: Re: Start lean with RAP (Restful ABAP programming model) #1
entry.content:
Hi Ivo, 

Thank you for your blog. I only used the RAP model with a behavior projection layer so far. I was very curious to try out your “lean approach” without a projection view and all the attributes in the behavior definition.

Unfortunately, this example doesn’t work for me. Everything activates without any errors, but no CRUD operations appear in the preview app of the V4 service.

I am using a customer table instead of I_PurchaseOrder in my root view entity, but I don’t think this should be the reason.

Is there anything I could have missed?
Best Regards,
Daniel

entry.title: How is the delivery plant determined in a sales order
entry.content:
There is a requirement to know how values for the delivery plant are determined when creating a sales order.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How is the delivery plant determined in a sales order
entry.content:
When processing an item, the system automatically attempts to determine the associated delivering plant from the master data. The entry can later be changed manually.

The system uses the following search strategy. It checks, in descending order, whether a specification exists:

  1. The customer-material info record.
  2. The customer master record of the ship-to party.
  3. The specification exists in the material master record.

If nothing is found, no delivering plant is set in the sales document item.

entry.title: Re: how to display fields before alv o/p like header data
entry.content: It's called an ALV Block List (function modules REUSE_ALV_BLOCK_LIST_*, or parameter IS_LAYOUT-LIST_APPEND='X' in the classic function module REUSE_ALV_LIST_DISPLAY). You may include anything you want between with WRITE.

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing - subscribe to MQTT topics
entry.content: hi, Under broker Basic Information, certificate chain should be uploaded for client or for server. Please clarify, SAP has not provided documentation.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to have Material tab together with TM reference in MIRO?
entry.content: Enter "02" in TM_INVOICE_CLERK parameter, you will be able to see Material TAB in MIRO.

entry.title: Re: how to display fields before alv o/p like header data
entry.content: Please embed the screenshots instead of hyperlink, it's easier to see all in one place:

entry.title: Re: S 4HANA Cloud 2408 mass asset retirement
entry.content: This question relates to https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('ABAVN')/S29PCE

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to have Material tab together with TM reference in MIRO?
entry.content: The user parameter has the following alternatives: Blank The user sees the TM Reference and PO Reference tabs, as well as the G/L Accounttab that is visible according to Customizing. 01 The user only sees the TM Reference tab and, depending on Customizing, the G/L Account tab. The PO Reference tab is hidden. 02 The user does not see the TM Reference tab, but sees the PO Reference tab, as well as all tabs that are visible according to Customizing (Material Account, G/L Account, and Contract Reference).

entry.title: Re: Archive Dunning
entry.content:
Dear All,
Quite an outdated question but as I came across the same issue recently and I am an archiving expert, I want to answer it.

The reason is that in the instruction the keys for SAP_OBJECT BUS3007 had been mixed up. The SAP_OBJECT definition for BUS3007 regarding the key fields is <BUKRS><CustomerNo> instead of <CustomerNo><BUKRS>. That's why archived documents cannot be found in the attachment list and also navigation to the corresponding customer on company code level TRX FD03 does not work via e.g. OAAD (Archived documents).

entry.title: Re: Abap how to display internal table content in the right container as a HTML page.
entry.content: Please use the buttons "..." and "</>" to display your code nicely, and also to let the people test and fix your code easily. See how to do it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/how-to-post-code-in-sap-community-gt-2024/qaq-p/13684774

entry.title: Re: Issue with Passing FREE SELECTIONS in SUBMIT Without VIA SELECTION-SCREEN
entry.content: FREE SELECTIONS!? Is ZTEST017 really using a logical database? The selections are passed to the logical database, but what your program ZTEST017 does with this data, people can't know if you don't describe it.

entry.title: Re: How to post code in SAP Community >=2024
entry.content: it will be corrected... someday.

entry.title: Data action validation error due to data access control in SAP Analytics cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Costcenter is enabled for data access control. 

If user having all costcenters access, i didnot see any data action validation error, but if the user having the one costcenter access then i am getting validation error. Below is my data action format.

how to overcome from this issue

 

Data action

memberset costcenter =%costcenter%

If cost center = A then 

1+2+3

ENDIF

IF COSTCENTER = B THEN

4+5+6

ENDIF

IF COSTCENTER = C THEN

7+8+9

ENDELSE

entry.title: Re: how to display fields before alv o/p like header data
entry.content: Could u please elaborate it ..i mean steps how to do it .

entry.title: Re: S 4HANA Cloud 2408 mass asset retirement
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Here's the What's New information about the deprecation of the app you mentioned.

The app you can use instead is called Post Asset Retirement (F7107).

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: How to setup Model View OData
entry.content:
Hi @emanuele_fumeo 

I aprociate your response, I found the mistake. there are In SAC client OAUTH in security it should be Credential client 

 

Regards,

 

Juan

entry.title: Re: Triggering SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from a Custom SAP Fiori Application

Thank you for sharing such an insightful blog. I attempted to implement this scenario on my trial account but encountered an error in my browser's network and console.

BTP Destination:

I need your guidance.
Thank you in advance

Manisha Jha

entry.title: Re: How to setup Model View OData
entry.content: Hi @juancortes, good that you found the issue.

entry.title: Re: Change Rate Italy Bank
entry.content: Did you reach use this API? Can you share details how to implemented it? Many thanks

entry.title: Missing F110 Payments in BNK_MONI
entry.content:
I created F110 payment proposal and payment run.  However when I go to BNK_MONI no payment information is shown.  BCM has been configured.  Please advise.

Thanks

CT
